---
# Token snapshot — mirrors the contract in app/globals.css (the implementation
# source of truth). Regenerated by /restyle on every apply. Format inspired by
# google-labs-code/design.md: machine-readable tokens + human rationale below.
name: Paper & Ink
default: light
themes: [light, dark]

colors:
  light:
    bg: "#e9e6df"
    surface: "#f1efe8"
    surface-raised: "#f7f5ef"
    surface-sunken: "rgba(26, 22, 16, 0.045)"
    text: "#1a1a18"
    text-body: "#26241f"
    text-muted: "#6a655b"
    text-faint: "#938d81"
    border: "#c7c1b4"
    border-soft: "rgba(26, 22, 16, 0.13)"
    border-faint: "rgba(26, 22, 16, 0.07)"
    accent: "#8f523a"
    accent-contrast: "#f7f5ef"
    accent-soft: "rgba(143, 82, 58, 0.12)"
  dark:
    bg: "#14130f"
    surface: "#1f1d18"
    surface-raised: "#262320"
    surface-sunken: "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3)"
    text: "#ece7dd"
    text-body: "#d7d1c5"
    text-muted: "#9c958a"
    text-faint: "#6e685e"
    border: "#38342d"
    border-soft: "rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.12)"
    border-faint: "rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.07)"
    accent: "#c67e5e"
    accent-contrast: "#14130f"
    accent-soft: "rgba(198, 126, 94, 0.16)"

fonts:
  display: "Hanken Grotesk"   # headlines, eyebrows, UI
  serif: "Fraunces"           # long-form / editorial prose
  body: "Hanken Grotesk"      # UI body text
  sans: "Hanken Grotesk"      # tracked labels / eyebrows
  mono: "Space Mono"          # code only

radius: { sm: 2px, md: 3px, lg: 4px, pill: 999px, circle: 50% }
borderWidth: { default: 1px, strong: 2px }
spacing: [4px, 8px, 12px, 16px, 24px, 32px, 48px, 64px, 80px]
type:
  xs: 11px
  sm: 13px
  base: 15px
  md: 17px
  lg: 22px
  xl: 28px
  title: "clamp(36px, 6vw, 56px)"
  subtitle: "clamp(16px, 2vw, 22px)"
shadow:
  sm: "none"
  md: "none"
  lg: "0 12px 40px rgba(26, 22, 16, 0.18)"   # true overlays only
motion:
  ease: "cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)"
  durations: { fast: 0.15s, base: 0.2s, slow: 0.6s }

# Frozen — consumed directly by isolated experiments (e.g. retro-tech); do NOT retheme.
frozen-aliases:
  light-bg: "#d8d4ca"
  light-text: "#2a2a28"
  light-rule: "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.15)"
  light-meta: "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5)"

# WCAG AA contrast results from the last /restyle lint (target: >=4.5:1 body, >=3:1 large/UI)
contrast:
  checked: true
  light:
    text-on-bg: 13.98
    body-on-bg: 12.44
    text-on-surface: 15.15
    text-on-raised: 15.99
    muted-on-bg: 4.65
    accent-on-bg: 4.90
    accent-contrast-on-accent: 5.61
  dark:
    text-on-bg: 15.08
    body-on-bg: 12.23
    text-on-surface: 13.66
    text-on-raised: 12.68
    muted-on-bg: 6.27
    accent-on-bg: 5.79
    accent-contrast-on-accent: 5.79
---

# Paper & Ink

The tokens above are the snapshot; this prose is the *intent* — why the values are what
they are and how to apply them. `/restyle` reads this before changing anything and rewrites
both halves on apply. `app/globals.css` is the implementation source of truth; if the two
ever disagree, globals.css wins and this file should be regenerated.

## Overview

A quiet, classic, editorial-Swiss system. **Light is the default**: a warm off-white paper
ground (greige, never pure white) under a soft near-black ink (never pure black). Color is
near-absent and reverential — a single muted clay/terracotta accent used only for links and
the live edge of a thing. Nothing glows; flat by default; hairline 1px rules and a near-square
2–4px radius do the structural work. Whitespace and the serif/sans contrast carry hierarchy.

Dark mode is a warm ink-on-near-black companion (same clay, lifted for the dark ground), used
both as the toggle state and as the palette for two **dark islands** that stay dark in either
site theme (see Architecture).

## Colors

- One accent, one source: `--accent` (muted clay). Never introduce a second hue — recolor the
  token. Tints/borders derive via `color-mix(... var(--accent) N% ...)`. The old stray pinks
  and the about report's purple were folded into this one accent.
- Text has four weights of presence: `--text` (headings/primary), `--text-body` (prose),
  `--text-muted` (meta), `--text-faint` (decoration). Don't reach for raw greys.
- Surfaces are nearly flat: `--bg` < `--surface` < `--surface-raised`; wells use
  `--surface-sunken`. Differences are subtle — rules separate, not fills.

## Typography

Swiss clarity + a modern serif revival. **Hanken Grotesk** carries display, UI, and the
tracked-uppercase eyebrows/labels (replacing the old mono meta). **Fraunces** (high-contrast
serif, italic for subtitles/meta) carries long-form reading. **Space Mono** is code only.
Headlines are grotesque at light-to-regular weight; let scale and the serif/sans contrast,
not many sizes, build hierarchy.

## Feel

Quiet and precise: near-square radius (sm 2 / md 3 / lg 4), hairline 1px borders, shadows
dropped to `none` except `--shadow-lg` reserved for true overlays (the nav drawer). Airy
spacing on the existing 4-based scale; one calm signature easing curve.

## Architecture (read before flipping themes)

The contract switches at any `[data-theme]` element, not only `:root`, so a subtree can be a
dark island while the rest of the page is paper. The dark island is now the **experiment
pages only**:

- **Experiment pages** — the route layout (`app/design-experiments/layout.tsx`) sets
  `data-theme="dark"` with `.layout` background `var(--bg)`, because the per-experiment frame
  leaves a gutter that falls through to it and the experiments were authored on (and look
  best against) a dark ground. That dark contrast when you open an experiment is intentional.
- **Gallery index** rides on top of that dark route layer but sets its OWN
  `data-theme="light"` container with a paper background (`app/design-experiments/page.module.css`
  `.container`), so the gallery reads light and uses the shared experiment card — cohesive
  with the home and blog indexes. Everything (home, blog, docs, news, about, gallery) is now
  paper; only the experiments themselves are dark.

The **home** is paper (dark ink on a solid paper ground — no background photo). The
`NetworkCanvas` node overlay renders on top; its colors are literals in `NetworkCanvas.tsx`
(canvas can't read CSS vars) — clay nodes over warm lines, kept from the dark treatment, so
the lines read quiet/faint on the light ground.

## Cards

Home, blog, and the gallery share one card treatment: a paper `--surface-raised` tile, hairline
`--border-faint` border with a flat clay-accent hover, near-square `--radius-lg` corners, a
16:9 sunken image well, a tracked grotesque date eyebrow, an ink display title, and a muted
serif-italic subtitle. The gallery reuses the actual `HomeExperimentCard` component (it takes
a `referrer`/`lastSlugKey` so back-nav + scroll-restore work from either home or gallery); the
blog index mirrors the same treatment in `blog.module.css`. Cards show the short `subtitle`,
never the long `description`.

## Do's and Don'ts

- DO change the look by editing the contract tokens first; only then go deeper into
  component CSS/markup.
- DO keep both themes legible — every text/background pair passes WCAG AA (see `contrast`).
- DON'T retheme the `frozen-aliases` (`--light-bg/-text/-rule/-meta`): isolated experiments
  (e.g. retro-tech) read them directly and must look unchanged.
- DON'T introduce a second accent hue, use pure black or pure white, or add glow/heavy shadow.
- DON'T hardcode a color, radius, or accent in a component when a token exists.
- DON'T touch `app/design-experiments/(experiments)/*` or `app/sketches/*` — they own their
  styling and must look unchanged after a restyle.
- DON'T light-theme the experiments room — it is an intentional dark island (gutter + the
  experiments were authored dark). The home, by contrast, is intentionally paper.
